title: Methods for manufacturing ceramic and ceramic composite components and components made thereby
abstract: Ceramic and ceramic composite components suitable for high temperature applications and methods of manufacturing such components. The components are formed by a displacive compensation of porosity (DCP) process and are suitable for use at operating temperatures above 600Â°C, and preferably above 1400Â°C, and possess superior mechanical properties.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A mechanically-robust ceramic or ceramic composite component for a high temperature system, the component prepared by a method comprising: reacting a fluid reactant and a porous preform that has a pore volume and contains a solid reactant compound that defines a solid volume of the porous preform, the fluid reactant comprising at least one displacing metal and the solid reactant compound of the preform having at least one displaceable species, the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant being capable of displacing the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound to produce at least one solid reaction product; and allowing the fluid reactant to infiltrate the porous preform and react with the solid reactant compound to produce the ceramic or ceramic composite component, during which the at least one displacing metal at least partially replaces the at least one displaceable species to produce the at least one solid reaction product that has a reaction product volume, the pore volume is at least partially filled by the reaction product volume, and the product volume is greater than the solid volume lost by the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound being displaced by the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant during the reaction of the fluid reactant and the solid reactant compound; and wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is more mechanically-robust than the porous preform. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component has a feature of a few millimeters or less and the porous preform possesses the feature of the component. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite 

 component has a feature of a few micrometers or less and the porous preform possesses the feature of the component. 4. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is coated with a layer of an oxidation-resistant and/or corrosion-resistant material. 5. The mechanically-robust component of claim 4, wherein the layer of the oxidation-resistant and/or corrosion-resistant material comprises a pure metal, a metal alloy, a ceramic, a ceramic alloy, a metal composite, a ceramic composite, or any combination thereof. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is a component of a system for a forming operation, a joining operation, a transportation system, an electrical power production system, a waste heat recovery system, a propulsion system, a national defense system, a chemical processing system, and a chemical and waste storage system. 7. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the component is chosen from the group consisting of dies, molds, blades, rollers, punches, bearings, gears, wheels, pulleys, drive train components, machining tools, grinding tools, welding tools, electromagnetic rail guns, gun barrel liners, pipes, containers, braking system components, power plant components, engine components, turbine blades, valves, pump components, pistons, compressors, and combustion chambers. 8. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the component is a ceramic/metal composite. 9. The mechanically-robust component of claim 8, wherein the ceramic of the ceramic/metal composite is a compound, solid solution, or mixture of one or more of a carbide, a boride, an oxide, a sulfide, a nitride, a halide, or any combination thereof. 10. The mechanically-robust component of claim 8, wherein the ceramic comprises one or more of carbides of aluminum, boron, chromium, hafnium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, scandium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, ytterbium, yttrium, and zirconium; borides of cobalt, chromium, hafnium, iron, lanthanum, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, neodymium, nickel, rhenium, rhodium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, ytterbium, and zirconium; nitrides of aluminum, boron, cerium, chromium, iron, hafnium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, silicon, tantalum, tin, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, zinc, and zirconium; oxides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, calcium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, hafnium, lithium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, potassium, silicon, sodium, strontium, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, and zirconium; and sulfides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, lanthanum, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, scandium, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, zinc, and zirconium. 1 1 . The mechanically-robust component of claim 8, wherein the metal of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of a metal, a metal solid solution, a metal mixture, or any combination thereof. 12. The mechanically-robust component of claim 8, wherein the metal of 

 the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of chromium, cobalt, copper, hafnium, iron, manganese, molybdenum, nickel, niobium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, yttrium, zirconium, or any combination thereof. 13. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is a friction stir welding tool and the porous preform possesses a shape of the friction stir welding tool. 14. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is a die and the porous preform possesses a shape of the die. 15. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is a turbine blade and the porous preform possesses a shape of the turbine blade. 16. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the solid reactant compound is WC, MoC, and/or Mo 2 C, the at least one displacing metal is Zr, the at least one displaceable species is W and/or Mo, and the at least one solid reaction product is ZrC. 17. The mechanically-robust component of claim 1 , wherein the mechanically-robust ceramic or ceramic composite component consists of the at least one displaceable species and the at least one solid reaction product. 18. The mechanically-robust component of claim 17, wherein the mechanically-robust ceramic or ceramic composite component consists of the at least one displaceable species is dispersed in the at least one solid reaction product. 19. A method for producing a mechanically-robust ceramic or ceramic composite component for a high temperature system, the method comprising: reacting a fluid reactant and a porous preform that has a pore volume and contains a solid reactant compound that defines a solid volume of the porous preform, the fluid reactant comprising at least one displacing metal and the solid reactant compound of the preform having at least one displaceable species, the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant being capable of displacing the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound to produce at least one solid reaction product; and allowing the fluid reactant to infiltrate the porous preform and react with the solid reactant compound to produce the ceramic or ceramic composite component, during which the at least one displacing metal at least partially replaces the at least one displaceable species to produce the at least one solid reaction product that has a reaction product volume, the pore volume is at least partially filled by the reaction product volume, and the product volume is greater than the solid volume lost by the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound being displaced by the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant during the reaction of the fluid reactant and the solid reactant compound; and wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is more mechanically-robust than the porous preform. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the component is a ceramic/metal composite. 21 . The method of claim 20, wherein the ceramic of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of a carbide, a boride, an oxide, a sulfide, a nitride, a halide, or any combination thereof. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein the ceramic comprises one or more of carbides of aluminum, boron, chromium, hafnium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, scandium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, ytterbium, yttrium, and zirconium; borides of cobalt, chromium, hafnium, iron, lanthanum, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, neodymium, nickel, rhenium, rhodium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, ytterbium, and zirconium; nitrides of aluminum, boron, cerium, chromium, iron, hafnium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, silicon, tantalum, tin, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, zinc, and zirconium; oxides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, calcium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, hafnium, lithium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, potassium, silicon, sodium, strontium, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, and zirconium; and sulfides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, lanthanum, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, scandium, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, zinc, and zirconium. 23. The method of claim 20, wherein the metal of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of a metal, a metal solid solution, a metal mixture, or any combination thereof. 24. The method of claim 20, wherein the metal of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of chromium, cobalt, copper, hafnium, iron, manganese, molybdenum, nickel, niobium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, yttrium, zirconium, or any combination thereof. 25. The method of claim 19, wherein the preform comprises one or more of the carbides of chromium, molybdenum, titanium, tungsten or one or more of the 

 borides of chromium, molybdenum, titanium, or tungsten and the fluid reactant is a liquid comprised of one or more of titanium, zirconium, or hafnium and one or more of copper, tin, nickel, cobalt, iron, lead, bismuth, manganese, cadmium, indium. 26. A high temperature system comprising a mechanically-robust ceramic or ceramic composite component prepared by a method comprising: reacting a fluid reactant and a porous preform that has a pore volume and contains a solid reactant compound that defines a solid volume of the porous preform, the fluid reactant comprising at least one displacing metal and the solid reactant compound of the preform having at least one displaceable species, the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant being capable of displacing the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound to produce at least one solid reaction product; and allowing the fluid reactant to infiltrate the porous preform and react with the solid reactant compound to produce the ceramic or ceramic composite component, during which the at least one displacing metal at least partially replaces the at least one displaceable species to produce the at least one solid reaction product that has a reaction product volume, the pore volume is at least partially filled by the reaction product volume, and the product volume is greater than the solid volume lost by the at least one displaceable species in the solid reactant compound being displaced by the at least one displacing metal of the fluid reactant during the reaction of the fluid reactant and the solid reactant compound; and wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is more mechanically-robust than the porous preform. 27. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the system is a system for a forming operation, a joining operation, a transportation system, an electrical power production system, a waste heat recovery system, a propulsion 

 system, a national defense system, a chemical processing system, or a chemical and waste storage system. 28. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the component is chosen from the group consisting of dies, molds, blades, rollers, punches, bearings, gears, wheels, pulleys, drive train components, machining tools, grinding tools, welding tools, electromagnetic rail guns, gun barrel liners, pipes, containers, braking system components, power plant components, engine components, turbine blades, valves, pump components, pistons, compressors, and combustion chambers. 30. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the component is a ceramic/metal composite. 31 . The high temperature system of claim 30, wherein the ceramic of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of a carbide, a boride, an oxide, a sulfide, a nitride, a halide, or any combination thereof. 32. The high temperature system of claim 30, wherein the ceramic comprises one or more of carbides of aluminum, boron, chromium, hafnium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, scandium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, ytterbium, yttrium, and zirconium; borides of cobalt, chromium, hafnium, iron, lanthanum, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, neodymium, nickel, rhenium, rhodium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, ytterbium, and zirconium; nitrides of aluminum, boron, cerium, chromium, iron, hafnium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, silicon, tantalum, tin, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, zinc, and zirconium; oxides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, calcium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, hafnium, lithium, magnesium, manganese, molybdenum, 

 niobium, nickel, potassium, silicon, sodium, strontium, tantalum, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, yttrium, and zirconium; and sulfides of aluminum, barium, bismuth, boron, cadmium, cerium, cesium, chromium, cobalt, copper, indium, iron, lanthanum, manganese, molybdenum, niobium, nickel, scandium, titanium, vanadium, tungsten, zinc, and zirconium. 33. The high temperature system of claim 30, wherein the metal of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of a metal, a metal solid solution, a metal mixture, or any combination thereof. 34. The high temperature system of claim 30, wherein the metal of the ceramic/metal composite comprises one or more of chromium, cobalt, copper, hafnium, iron, manganese, molybdenum, nickel, niobium, silicon, tantalum, titanium, tungsten, vanadium, yttrium, zirconium, or any combination thereof. 35. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the preform comprises one or more of the carbides of chromium, molybdenum, titanium, tungsten or one or more of the borides of chromium, molybdenum, titanium, or tungsten and the fluid reactant is a liquid comprised of one or more of titanium, zirconium, or hafnium and one or more of copper, tin, nickel, cobalt, iron, lead, bismuth, manganese, cadmium, indium. 36. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the component is a friction stir welding tool and the porous preform possesses a shape of the friction stir welding tool. 37. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the component is a die and the porous preform possesses a shape of the die. 38. The high temperature system of claim 26, wherein the ceramic or ceramic composite component is a turbine blade and the porous preform possesses a shape of the turbine blade.
